LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT XCOTTON CO. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO BE HEARD ON DECEMBER 18, 2025. IF THE PARTIES DO NOT APPLY FOR COMPLEX DESIGNATION, THEY ARE ORDERED TO SUBMIT A JOINT STATEMENT EXPLAINING THEIR REASONING 7 DAYS PRIOR TO THE HEARING WITH A COURTESY COPY DELIVERED TO THIS DEPARTMENT WITHIN 24 HOURS OF FILING. THE COURT MAY SUA SPONTE DESIGNATE A CASE AS COMPLEX UNDER CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.400 ET SEQ. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT BY DEFENDANT XCOTTON
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT XCOTTON CO. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Xcotton Co.'s demurrer is OFF CALENDAR. The court on its own motion designates this case complex under CRC 3.400 et seq. It is hereby ordered that this action is singly assigned for all purposes to the Complex Litigation Department 304, the Hon. Ethan P. Schulman presiding. Department 304 will issue a subsequent order regarding scheduling and complex department procedures.

The moving party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2025 Continued To Dec-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT XCOTTON CO. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Continued on the court's own motion to be heard on December 18, 2025. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding complex case designation and make any appropriate application to the court. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.400; L.R.S.F., rule 3.5(c)(2).) If the parties do not apply for complex designation, they are ordered to submit a joint statement explaining their reasoning 7 days prior to the hearing with a courtesy copy delivered to this department within 24 hours of filing. The Court may sua sponte designate a case as complex under California Rules of Court, rule 3.400 et seq.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
